name: AWS Elastic Container Registry (ECR)
what_it_does: Fully managed container image registry from AWS that stores, manages, and deploys container images with deep IAM integration, lifecycle policies, and vulnerability scanning, accessed via AWS SDK and CLI.
use_cases: Storing and retrieving container images as part of AWS-based CI/CD pipelines
Automating image lifecycle policies to delete old tags and control storage costs
Pulling vulnerability scan results for images using ECR Enhanced Scanning
Granting cross-account image access for multi-account AWS organizations
Triggering deployment workflows on image push via EventBridge integration
not_for: Multi-cloud container registry (ECR is AWS-specific, not portable)
Organizations not on AWS — cost and integration overhead not justified
Public image hosting at scale (Docker Hub or GHCR are better for public images)
best_when: Your workloads run on AWS (ECS, EKS, Lambda containers) and you want a tightly integrated, IAM-governed container registry with no rate limits.
avoid_when: You're not on AWS, need multi-cloud portability, or want to host public images with easy external access.

auth.notes: Uses AWS IAM for all authentication and authorization. Docker login tokens are obtained via GetAuthorizationToken and expire after 12 hours. IAM policies control per-resource permissions with fine granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 500MB/month storage for 12 months (new accounts); ECR Public is free for public images
pricing.paid_tiers: Private: $0.10/GB/month storage + data transfer costs; Public Gallery: free for public images
pricing.notes: Costs are typically low for most organizations. Large image catalogs or high-frequency CI/CD with large images can add up.

performance.rate_limits: GetAuthorizationToken: 20 TPS; most other operations: 100-500 TPS depending on action

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS SDK errors include error code and message. ECR-specific errors (RepositoryNotFoundException, ImageNotFoundException) are descriptive and easy to handle.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Repository creation fails with RepositoryAlreadyExistsException (not idempotent by default). Image push is idempotent — pushing same digest skips upload. Lifecycle policies are idempotent PutLifecyclePolicy calls.
known_agent_gotchas: Docker auth tokens from GetAuthorizationToken expire after 12 hours — agents must refresh before token expiry
ECR registry URLs are account-and-region-specific (123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com) — not portable
Cross-account access requires both a resource-based policy on the repository AND IAM permission for the calling account
Repository creation is not idempotent by default — check existence before creating or handle the exception
Image manifest operations (put/get) use Docker Registry HTTP API V2, not the ECR AWS API — requires separate auth

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM policies for ECR push/pull permissions. Image scanning for vulnerability detection. Immutable image tags to prevent overwrite. Cross-account access via resource policies. Container image signing with AWS Signer. SOC2, FedRAMP.
